questions.statement: The domain of the function given by 2<sup>x</sup> + 2<sup>y</sup> = 2 is

Let <span class="math-tex">\(f(x)=x^{2}\)</span> and <span class="math-tex">\(g(x)=\sin x\)</span> for all <span class="math-tex">\(x\in \mathbb{R}\)</span>. Then the set of all <span class="math-tex">\(x\)</span> satisfying <span class="math-tex">\((f\circ g\circ g\circ f)(x)=(g\circ g\circ f)(x)\)</span>, where <span class="math-tex">\((f\circ g)(x)=f(g(x))\)</span>, is

options: f is both one-one and onto /
